assessment_items.question_content: **San Vitale has an unusual plan. It is a centrally-planned  [[☃ dropdown 1]] church.**
hints:
**San Vitale is unusual in its design. It is a centrally-planned octagonal church instead of the more typical basilica church.**

![Floor plan of San Vitale showing its unusual centrally planned octagonal design with a circular core surrounded by radiating spaces, differing from the typical rectangular basilica layout.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95efa7ae3c8e3e2473a579cee086ff5a.jpg)

assessment_items.question_content:
**The columns in San Vitale incorporate . . .**


![Ornate column capitals decorated with carved foliage patterns and reliefs of lambs bearing crosses, supporting arches with intricate geometric and floral designs.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51f98fc361344fad3be648c368511133.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The columns and capitals at San Vitale do not conform to earlier architectural styles and incorporate Christian symbolism.**

choices.content: Greek orders from Ancient Greek architecture (Ionic, Doric, and Corinthian)
the Tuscan order from Ancient Roman architecture
Egyptian columns reminiscent of hypostyle halls

assessment_items.question_content:
**The mosaics of Justinian and Theodora remind the viewer . . .**

![Mosaic showing a central haloed ruler, Justinian, in a jeweled crown and robes holding a golden bowl, flanked by clergy in white garments and attendants, with soldiers on the left carrying shields and spears, all set against a gold background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7ab11136f240116bc278adfd52c77452.jpg)

![Mosaic showing a haloed empress, Theodora, in elaborate jeweled robes and crown holding a golden chalice, flanked by attendants and courtiers, set against a gold background with architectural details.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/87ef373753a0d2f1a9c317192228c43a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **The images of Justinian and Theodora serve as a reminder of distant rulers who promote Orthodox Christianity.**

choices.content: of the power of rulers who live in distant Constantinople
of the power of local rulers

assessment_items.question_content:
**What makes the representation of Christ in the apse of San Vitale classical in appearance?**


![Mosaic of a beardless Christ with a cruciform halo, wearing a brown robe and sandals, seated on a blue orb and flanked by four white-robed angels against a gold background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/73deb77af4484039cdecb9584c863bf7.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **A representation of Christ *with* a beard is a medieval, as opposed to more classicizing, representation of Christ.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Match the visual detail with its meaning in the mosaic program at San Vitale. **

 

[[☃ matcher 1]]
hints: Although Justinian probably never visited San Vitale, his is shown holding a paten used in the ritual of the Eucharist, which would have been performed in the sanctuary.
The Chi Rho on the shield in front of Justinian's soldiers indicates the they serve Christ.
Both Theodora and Justinian are surrounded by halos, showing their power derives from divine authority.

The spatial ambiguity in the placement of Justinian and Maxentius may reflect existing tensions between Imperial and Church power.  

**[Read this text to review](/humanities/medieval-world/byzantine1/venice-ravenna/a/san-vitale)**

left: ![Mosaic detail of hands holding a gold, jewel-patterned bowl](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0facdb61a19712b0a84b3d240c0e5b2a.jpg)

![Mosaic portraits showing a man and a woman with golden halos, richly adorned with jeweled crowns and garments, set against a gold background.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0b35a3d3997d152e1c621e86ae3d4b6.jpg)
![Mosaic detail of a large blue-green shield decorated with a jeweled gold cross design, held by a soldier.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a758683689a24911e34b267afcc8558.jpg)

![Mosaic showing a richly dressed ruler holding a golden bowl, flanked by clergy in white robes, including a bishop holding a jeweled cross.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/12bec531f41c7041a2f7ad566a761dd7.jpg)
right: Justinian participates in the ritual of the Eucharist.
Justinian and Theodora's power is derived from divine authority.
Tensions may exist between Imperial power and Church authority.
